whatItMeasures: Homocysteine is an amino acid produced during the metabolism of methionine; it is normally recycled back to methionine (requiring vitamin B12 and folate) or converted to cysteine (requiring B6). Elevated blood levels are associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk and can indicate nutritional deficiencies.
causesOfHigh: Vitamin B12 deficiency (most common nutritional cause)
Homocystinuria (rare inherited enzyme deficiency causing very high levels)
MTHFR gene variants (reduce folate metabolism efficiency)
causesOfLow: Low homocysteine is not a clinical concern
High folate, B12, or B6 intake lowers homocysteine

high: Mildly elevated homocysteine typically causes no direct symptoms
In homocystinuria (very high levels): dislocated eye lenses, tall stature with long limbs, osteoporosis, blood clots, and intellectual disability
Elevated homocysteine is associated with increased long-term cardiovascular risk: heart attack and stroke
May be associated with increased dementia risk in older adults
